svg.text: IRS Form 8027 — Employer's Annual Information Return of Tip Income
Annual filing requirement for large food and beverage establishments · Tax Year 2025
Required for large food/beverage establishments (annual gross receipts >$1M OR employs >10 tipped employees on a typical day).
Due: February 28 of the following year (March 31 if e-filing). Failure to file or underreporting = 20% excise tax on allocated tips.
Total charge receipts (food+beverage on which tips were charged)
Total service charges paid as wages (auto-grat)
Total tips reported by tipped employees
Allocated tips (if total reported < 8% × gross receipts)
✓ No allocation needed — reported tips exceed 8% threshold
Number of tipped employees on a typical business day
Did you file Form 8027 for calendar year 2024?
← EXCEEDS minimum ✅ — No tip allocation required
PER-EMPLOYEE ALLOCATIONS (would apply if total < 8% threshold)
No allocation required. If gross receipts > $1M and reported tips fall below 8%, allocation would be
prorated to each server: (server gross rcpts / total gross rcpts) × (8% × total gross rcpts − tips reported)
Reported $96,540 > $67,392 → formula yields $0 for all employees.
✓ No allocation required for tax year 2025.
Reconciled with Gusto/ADP W-2 Box 7 (tip fields) annually.
Last generated: Jan 15, 2026 · Not yet e-filed · Due Feb 28, 2026
Downtown Burger Co. · EIN: 47-XXXXXXX · Form 8027 — Tax Year 2025 · Synalux POS v4.2
